name: Mississippi County, Arkansas Economic Opportunity Commission, Inc.
description_short: Mississippi County Economic Opportunity Commission provides free Community Action services including tax assistance, housing support, and energy assistance programs for low-income families.
description_long:
Mississippi County, Arkansas Economic Opportunity Commission, Inc. (MCAEOC) is a non-profit Community Action agency serving Mississippi County and surrounding areas. The organization operates under the mission "Helping People to Help Themselves" and provides essential services to low-income residents through federal and state funding. As a Community Action agency, MCAEOC is part of a national network dedicated to alleviating poverty and expanding economic opportunity.

MCAEOC offers a comprehensive suite of free and low-cost services including Head Start programs for early childhood development, the Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program (LIHEAP) for utility bill assistance, the Commodity Supplemental Food Program for nutrition support, and housing assistance programs. The organization also operates the Arkansas Volunteer Income Tax Assistance (VITA) program for the 2025-26 tax year, providing free tax preparation and filing services to eligible low-income households. These services are delivered through multiple Neighborhood Service Centers located throughout the county.

What distinguishes MCAEOC is its integrated approach to poverty alleviation, addressing multiple dimensions of economic hardship simultaneously—from childhood education and nutrition to energy costs and housing stability. As a federally-designated Community Action agency, the organization maintains accountability to both government funders and the communities it serves. The VITA program indicates volunteer income tax assistance capabilities, suggesting some coordination with IRS-certified tax professionals or volunteers.

The primary limitation is that MCAEOC does not offer credit-building products, credit repair services, or debt management programs. The website provides limited detail on income eligibility thresholds, application processes, or specific service availability. As a regional agency, service availability and program specifics may vary by location within Mississippi County. Prospective clients should contact their local Neighborhood Service Center directly for detailed program information and eligibility verification.
pros: Free tax preparation services through Arkansas VITA program (2025-26)
Multiple Neighborhood Service Centers for convenient local access
Comprehensive approach addressing energy costs, food security, and housing simultaneously
Federal Community Action agency status ensures accountability and standardized operations
Head Start programs provide early childhood education for low-income families
Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program helps with utility bills
No-cost food assistance through Commodity Supplemental Food Program
cons: Website lacks detailed information on income eligibility limits and application procedures
No credit counseling or financial education services mentioned despite free-help categorization
Limited transparency on program waitlists, availability, or service delivery timelines
Website does not clarify which services are fully free versus those with sliding-scale fees
No online application portal or digital service options described
best_for: Low-income families seeking free tax preparation and filing assistance
Households struggling with heating/cooling costs through LIHEAP
Families with young children needing Head Start early education programs
Low-income individuals requiring emergency food and nutrition support
diagnosis: MCAEOC is best for low-income Mississippi County residents seeking free federal antipoverty services, particularly tax preparation, utility assistance, food support, and early childhood programs. The main caveat is that this is not a credit-focused organization—it does not offer credit repair, credit building, debt management, or credit counseling services despite the free-help categorization.
services: Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program (LIHEAP)
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
